title: Treehouse Of Horror X

jokes.text: if you haven't been probed by these two... you haven't been probed
jokes.setup: Traditional talk show introduction format
jokes.punchline: Alien probe joke as casual accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Solid opening gag that plays with alien stereotype while maintaining talk show format

jokes.text: He had to borrow a human brain
jokes.setup: Kodos apologizing for Kang's behavior
jokes.punchline: The 'excuse' is that he's using an inferior human brain
jokes.explanation: Great character moment that establishes alien superiority complex while explaining bad hosting

jokes.text: I still can't believe we escaped from those horrible vampires. But it was worth it to get back our Super Sugar Crisp cereal
jokes.setup: Dramatic escape from vampires sounds serious
jokes.punchline: The quest was for breakfast cereal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating cereal as worth risking life for

jokes.text: # Guess I forgot to put the fog lights in #
jokes.setup: Marge asking about fog lights in dangerous conditions
jokes.punchline: Homer casually singing about his negligence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant musical moment that turns Homer's incompetence into a jaunty tune

jokes.text: Please be a dog
jokes.setup: Homer has clearly hit something with the car
jokes.punchline: His desperate hope for the least bad outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Homer's selfish desperation in crisis

jokes.text: He's definitely dead! Oh, my God. We killed Ned Flanders
jokes.setup: Checking if Ned is dead
jokes.punchline: Marge's increasingly frantic confirmation with eye squishing sounds
jokes.explanation: Dark humor enhanced by grotesque sound effects

jokes.text: You mean you killed Ned Flanders
jokes.setup: Homer saying 'we' killed Ned
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately distancing herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - moral but also self-preserving

jokes.text: And if anyone asks you, just say that you are not dead
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his plan to hide Ned's death
jokes.punchline: His solution is to have the corpse deny being dead
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer logic - brilliant in its complete absurdity and confidence

jokes.text: [ Imitating Ned ] Hi, Maude. Diddly. I've been having fun with my pal Homer. Diddly
jokes.setup: Homer needs to make dead Ned appear alive
jokes.punchline: His terrible ventriloquism with corpse
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy with Homer's bad impression skills

jokes.text: But when I do die, I don't want any autopsies
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to be living Ned
jokes.punchline: Immediately brings up death and avoiding investigation
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the worst possible thing to say while impersonating a dead person

jokes.text: [ Imitating Ned ] Hey, Maude. I'm home. Uh-oh! I think I'm having a heart attack!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to impersonate dead Ned
jokes.punchline: Faking Ned's death to explain why he won't be moving anymore
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the cover-up - Homer solving one problem by creating another

jokes.text: When I think about Ned, I can't help but remember the look on his face when Marge drove over-
jokes.setup: Homer giving eulogy at Ned's funeral
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts confessing to the crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer inability to keep secrets, even at the worst moment

jokes.text: Anyway, in conclusion, a man cannot be forced to testify against his wife
jokes.setup: Homer trying to recover from his near-confession
jokes.punchline: Misapplying legal concepts in a eulogy
jokes.explanation: Homer's TV-law knowledge applied inappropriately at a funeral

jokes.text: Hey. We just got away with murder. And it was so easy!
jokes.setup: Successfully covering up Ned's death
jokes.punchline: Homer's cheerful pride in the crime
jokes.explanation: Dark but characteristic Homer celebrating the wrong thing

jokes.text: You know, I've never liked that little wiener Milhouse
jokes.setup: Homer realizing murder is easy
jokes.punchline: Immediately targeting another victim
jokes.explanation: Homer's escalation from accidental to intentional murder, targeting a child

jokes.text: Oh! Hey, Homer. It's Moe. I must have dialed the wrong number
jokes.setup: Scary threatening phone call building tension
jokes.punchline: It's just Moe with wrong number
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection but fairly predictable horror movie subversion

jokes.text: And I'll go skinny-dipping in that lake... where the sexy teens were killed a hundred years ago tonight
jokes.setup: Family splitting up to hide from killer
jokes.punchline: Homer chooses the most obviously dangerous horror movie location
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about horror movie clichés combined with Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: You can't kill the undead, silly
jokes.setup: Homer confused that Ned is alive after killing him
jokes.punchline: Ned cheerfully explains basic undead rules
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Ned's characteristic patience in explaining the obvious

jokes.text: # Guess I forgot to put the fog lights in #
jokes.setup: Same car accident scenario in flashback
jokes.punchline: Homer singing the same tune about his negligence
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that reinforces Homer's casual attitude toward dangerous negligence

jokes.text: Hey, foamy. I want the news, not the weather
jokes.setup: Werewolf Ned is presumably howling/growling
jokes.punchline: Homer treating monster sounds like bad TV forecasting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer line - treating werewolf transformation like annoying TV programming

jokes.text: Diddly!
jokes.setup: Ned has transformed into a werewolf
jokes.punchline: Still uses his characteristic speech pattern while growling
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Ned's politeness persists even as a monster

jokes.text: Eyes bigger than your stomach, eh, wolfie?
jokes.setup: Werewolf Ned has presumably bitten off more than he can chew
jokes.punchline: Homer using dinner table wisdom for werewolf attack
jokes.explanation: Good Homer line applying domestic sayings to supernatural situations

jokes.text: Hey, you know, we got feelings too, Chief
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum calling officer the cutest in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Officer's sensitive response to being objectified
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of macho cop stereotype with vulnerability

jokes.text: I don't think the real Radioactive Man wears a plastic smock with a picture of himself on it. He would on Halloween
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Bart's costume authenticity
jokes.punchline: Bart's illogical but confident defense
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with Bart's circular Halloween logic

jokes.text: She's been crushed. And so have the hopes of our Mathletics team
jokes.setup: Lisa appears to be crushed by X-ray machine
jokes.punchline: Skinner equally concerned about academic competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - treating academic concerns as equal to human life

jokes.text: I must only use this power to annoy
jokes.setup: Bart discovering he has stretching superpowers
jokes.punchline: His immediate moral code is mischief-focused
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart philosophy - getting superpowers and immediately planning pranks

jokes.text: Well, whenever you notice something like that, a wizard did it
jokes.setup: Fan asking about continuity error in comic
jokes.punchline: Dismissive catch-all explanation for plot holes
jokes.explanation: Legendary meta-humor about lazy writing - became internet meme for plot holes

jokes.text: Wizard. Ah, for glavin out loud.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy starts another complaint
jokes.punchline: Immediately shut down with 'wizard' plus his made-up exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with Comic Book Guy's frustrated reaction

jokes.text: Oh, please. I'm not insane. I simply wish to take you back to my lair and make you my bride
jokes.setup: Being accused of being insane
jokes.punchline: Casually describing obviously insane behavior as reasonable
jokes.explanation: Good villain logic - complete lack of self-awareness about obvious insanity

jokes.text: It was fired only once.. to keep William Shatner from making another album
jokes.setup: Describing his unique phaser weapon
jokes.punchline: Its one use was stopping Shatner's singing career
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture burn that justifies sci-fi weapon with real-world application

jokes.text: Obi-Wan. Iron Man. Mr. Mxyzptlk. And, of course, Big Papa Smurf
jokes.setup: Listing impressive names for wedding night roleplay
jokes.punchline: Ending with ridiculous Smurf reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from cool characters to absurd cartoon reference

jokes.text: Come here, you. Xena needs "xex."
jokes.setup: Seducing the Collector to escape
jokes.punchline: Terrible Xena-themed sexual pun
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad pun that works through its awfulness

jokes.text: What do you think I've been trying to do?
jokes.setup: Lisa telling Bart to drop her and save himself
jokes.punchline: Bart admitting he's been trying to drop her
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling relationship humor - Bart's honest selfishness

jokes.text: You removed it from its original packaging! It's no longer a collectible!
jokes.setup: Using rare light saber as weapon
jokes.punchline: Collector's horror at losing collectible value
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nerd humor - perfect collector's nightmare scenario

jokes.text: Must end life in classic Lorne Greene pose... from Battle star Galactica. Best... death... ever!
jokes.setup: Collector dying in Lucite
jokes.punchline: Choosing dramatic TV pose and rating his own death
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd death - treating final moments like collectible event

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Xena can't fly. I told you. I'm not Xena. I'm Lucy Lawless
jokes.setup: Xena flying them to safety
jokes.punchline: Actress breaking character to explain she's not the character
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about actor/character distinction

jokes.text: We're not White snake, dude. We're Poison. I thought we were Quiet Riot. It says here we're Ratt
jokes.setup: Dick Clark introducing wrong band
jokes.punchline: Band members equally confused about their own identity
jokes.explanation: Good satire of interchangeable 80s hair metal bands

jokes.text: Man alive! What a stinko thousand years. Blimp wrecks. Teenagers.
jokes.setup: Dick Clark reflecting on the millennium
jokes.punchline: Treats major disasters and teenagers as equally annoying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for ageless Dick Clark with funny priority confusion

jokes.text: Doing what now?
jokes.setup: Being asked about fixing Y2K computer bugs
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete ignorance of his supposed job
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence reveal at worst possible moment

jokes.text: That can't be true, honey. If it were, I'd be terrified
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how computer failure could end world
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular logic avoiding responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - assuming he'd know if he should be scared

jokes.text: We want to thank Dixie brand mayonnaise. Whoo, Lordy, what a mayonnaise!
jokes.setup: New Year's Eve celebration
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic corporate sponsorship acknowledgment for mayonnaise
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment about inappropriate New Year's sponsors

jokes.text: Who's gonna clean up all those jets?
jokes.setup: Planes falling from sky due to computer failure
jokes.punchline: Homer's mundane concern about cleanup logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer response - treating apocalypse like minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Well, those ivory-tower eggheads have screwed us again
jokes.setup: World ending due to Homer's incompetence
jokes.punchline: Blaming intellectuals for his failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflection - blaming smart people for his own mistakes

jokes.text: Hey. This milk's broken. It's got a computer chip in it
jokes.setup: Everything having computer chips in Y2K world
jokes.punchline: Homer's description of high-tech milk as 'broken'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer technological confusion - treating advancement as malfunction

jokes.text: I resisted these for 35 years. Why did I wear them today?
jokes.setup: Reverend Lovejoy blaming fashion for apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Marge's timing regret about finally wearing go-go boots
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's fashion regret at worst possible time

jokes.text: But I was gonna loot you a present
jokes.setup: Marge not wanting Bart to participate in looting
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent motivation for criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - making crime seem thoughtful and sweet

jokes.text: I don't want you looting. But I was gonna loot you a present.
jokes.setup: Marge forbidding looting during chaos
jokes.punchline: Bart frames theft as gift-giving
jokes.explanation: Cute family dynamic with Bart's manipulative logic about theft as gift-giving

jokes.text: Wonders, Lisa? Or blunders?
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on computer age wonders
jokes.punchline: Homer's forced rhyming contradiction
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but fits Homer's tendency toward forced wordplay

jokes.text: Implied, Lisa? Or implode?
jokes.setup: Lisa saying his pun was implied
jokes.punchline: Homer doubling down with worse pun
jokes.explanation: Even weaker pun but shows Homer's stubborn commitment to bad wordplay

jokes.text: Nectar. Nectar. I need to drink my weight in nectar
jokes.setup: Krusty's pacemaker stuck on hummingbird speed
jokes.punchline: Acting like actual hummingbird with dietary needs
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist medical emergency with animal behavior mimicry

jokes.text: I can still hear his voice on the wind
jokes.setup: Krusty saying he's not dead
jokes.punchline: Homer's melodramatic mourning for living person
jokes.explanation: Good Homer dramatic overreaction to non-death

jokes.text: Certainly. I am... the, uh, piano genius from the movie Shine. Uh, Shiny McShine
jokes.setup: Homer trying to pass as intellectual for Mars trip
jokes.punchline: His terrible fake name for famous pianist
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer fake identity with hilariously obvious made-up name

jokes.text: Remember me as I am- filled with murderous rage!
jokes.setup: Homer's goodbye to Lisa
jokes.punchline: His self-description emphasizes worst qualities
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer farewell - touching moment ruined by honest self-assessment

jokes.text: And I could've, 'cause I'm a big guy, and I'm good with knots
jokes.setup: Tom Arnold defending his TV shows
jokes.punchline: Admitting he could have physically forced people to watch
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity roast with surprisingly dark confession

jokes.text: The sun? That's the hottest place on Earth. Gonna work on my "tannage," buddy
jokes.setup: Learning their ship is headed for the sun
jokes.punchline: Treating solar death as tanning opportunity with Pauly Shore slang
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction with Homer's optimism and Shore's dated slang

jokes.text: Oh, no. Rosie O'Donnell! Come on, everybody! Sing along!
jokes.setup: Various celebrities revealed on doomed ship
jokes.punchline: Rosie O'Donnell forcing group singing makes it even worse
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke about forced cheer making doom worse

jokes.text: Don't worry, Dad. We'll be dead in five minutes. Not fast enough!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to comfort Homer about Rosie O'Donnell singing
jokes.punchline: Homer preferring death to musical torture
jokes.explanation: Strong closer - Homer finding forced entertainment worse than death

tmdb_overview: In "I Know What You Diddily-Iddly-Did" the Simpsons fear they have killed Ned Flanders. "Desperately Xeeking Xena" satirizes superheroes of yore with "Stretch Dude" Bart and "Clobber Girl" Lisa. And "Life's A Glitch, Then You Die" depicts the upcoming horror of Y2K.
